title: Rural Independent Power Producers: Who and What Does It Take?
summary: Rural Independent Power Producers: Who and What Does It Take? Independent power producers, also known as IPPs, have brought hundreds of kilowatts of renewable power onto electric grids throughout Alaska, with plans for hundreds of megawatts more. These non-utility companies are often private, for-profit entities that generate and sell power to electric utilities through power purchase agreements. But in rural Alaska, where markets typically aren’t large enough to entice private investment, c...
